content: IMU packet structure for UDP-like delivery: Use a compact binary header: uint16 sequence_number (0-65535 wrap), uint32 or float64 timestamp_ms (phone's performance.now() at sample time), uint8 flags (gesture bits, touch state). Payload: quaternion as 4x float32 = 16 bytes, optional linear_accel as 3x float16 = 6 bytes, optional position_delta as 3x float16 = 6 bytes. Total typical packet ~30-40 bytes. Sequence number used to discard stale packets on receive: if incoming seq < last_received_seq (accounting for wrap), drop it. Never buffer — always render the latest received state. Jitter buffer pattern: maintain a small ring buffer of 2-3 slots, pick the one with the newest seq/timestamp for rendering. Interpolation: lerp between last two received quaternions scaled by delta-time for smooth render when packets are late.
